ccel.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

title: To John, Bishop of Syracuse.

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: I have received your Fraternity’s letter, wherein you inform me that the most eloquent Martin has come from the African province and communicated something to you privately.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And indeed your Fraternity, as often as you find occasion, ceases not to shew your love towards the blessed apostle Peter.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Wherefore we give thanks to Almighty God, that where you are, there we are not found absent.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Nevertheless, your Holiness is not yet fully cognizant of the case in hand.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For the Byzacene primate had been accused on some charge, and the most pious Emperor wished him to be judged by us according to canonical ordinance.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But then, on the receipt of ten pounds of gold, Theodorus the magister militum opposed this being done.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Yet the most pious Emperor admonished us to commission some one, and do whatever was canonical.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, seeing the contrarieties of men, we have been unwilling to decide this case.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Now, moreover, this same primate says something about his own intention.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And it is exceedingly doubtful whether he says such things to us sincerely, or in fact because he is being attacked by his fellow-bishops: for, as to his saying that he is subject to the Apostolic See, if any fault is found in bishops, I know not what bishop is not subject to it.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But when no fault requires it to be otherwise, all according to the principle of humility are equal.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Nevertheless, do you speak with the aforesaid most eloquent Martin as seems good to your Fraternity.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For it is for you to consider what should be done; and we have replied to you briefly on the case, because we ought not to believe indiscriminately men that are even unknown to us.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: If, however, you, who see him before you in person, are of opinion that anything more definite should be said to him, we commit this to your Charity, being sure of your love in the grace of Almighty God.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And what you do regard without doubt as having been done by us.
